title: Executive Order 13115—Interagency Task Force on the Roles and Missions of the United States Coast Guard

flags.justification: A broad, fourteen-agency task force with purely advisory review-and-recommendation functions.
A required written report, specific methodology requirements (Sec. 3), and reporting through the Secretary of Transportation to the President.
Multiple related review functions all coherently serve one purpose: reviewing Coast Guard roles and missions.
An advisory review task force with no compliance regime.
No compliance regime exists for an incentive structure to attach to.
Sec. 5(a) sets an explicit termination: six months from the Task Force's first meeting, unless extended.
This creates a new review structure rather than removing any existing oversight.
A broad, multi-agency advisory task force with no third-party compliance regime.

Reviews an important, ongoing institutional question with real downstream budget implications, explicitly tied to informing investment levels in the Coast Guard's major 'Deepwater' procurement program.

Sec. 3(e) explicitly bounds the review's scope, requiring the Task Force to assume the Coast Guard will remain a law enforcement agency and armed force — a protective boundary rather than an open-ended mandate.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (negative). Structural weight: 1/14 = 7.1%. The final order in this 298-EO corpus, coded to complete the full Mayer & Price negative-class validation sample. A well-designed, time-limited, appropriately bounded interagency review task force.
